skill.name: amazon-competitor-analyzer
skill.description: Scrapes Amazon product data from ASINs using browseract.com automation API and performs surgical competitive analysis. Compares specifications, pricing, review quality, and visual strategies to identify competitor moats and vulnerabilities.
skill.summary: Automated Amazon competitive analysis tool that extracts product data via browser automation and generates comparative reports.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: This skill scrapes Amazon product data via the BrowserAct API and generates competitive analysis reports. The static scanner found 86 potential issues including network access, environment variable reading, filesystem writes, and shell command patterns. After AI evaluation: network calls go to a single hardcoded BrowserAct API endpoint (no exfiltration), env access follows standard API key patterns, filesystem writes are legitimate report output, and shell commands in SKILL.md are documentation-only (markdown code blocks, not executed). The skill combines multiple high-risk capabilities (network + credentials + filesystem) which requires attention, but the code is transparent with no obfuscation or malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: Manual Environment File Parsing without Validation
medium_findings.description: When python-dotenv is unavailable, the script manually parses .env files line-by-line (lines 34-39). This pattern reads credential data without input validation or error handling for malformed entries, and exposes the file path in error messages.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual .env parsing is a less secure pattern than library-based loading but is commonly used in Python scripts. The code filters comments and empty lines, mitigating basic injection risks.

medium_findings.title: API Key Exposure via Command-Line Argument
medium_findings.description: The script accepts an API key via the -k command-line flag (line 345). CLI arguments are visible in process listings and shell history, which can expose credentials to other users on shared systems.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Well-known OS-level credential exposure vector. CLI arguments are visible to all users via /proc filesystem on Linux. Environment variables are the preferred approach.

medium_findings.title: Heuristic Dangerous Capability Combination
medium_findings.description: The skill combines network access, credential access, filesystem write operations, and code execution capabilities. While the current implementation is transparent and legitimate, this capability combination could be misused if the code were modified.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static analyzer correctly identified the capability combination as dangerous. However, AI review confirms the code is transparent, single-purpose, and shows no obfuscation or exfiltration patterns.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External API
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com using the requests library. All URLs are hardcoded with no dynamic URL construction, reducing risk of SSRF or redirection attacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls are to a single hardcoded HTTPS endpoint. No data exfiltration detected. All API interactions are necessary for the skill's function.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write Operations for Report Output
low_findings.description: The script writes CSV, Markdown, and JSON reports to the local filesystem using open() and os.makedirs(). Output path is user-controlled via the -o flag.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File writes are legitimate report generation for user-specified output directories. No patterns of data staging, hidden files, or non-standard locations detected.

low_findings.title: External Command Patterns in Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md file contains shell command examples in markdown code blocks. The static analyzer detected 16 instances of backtick execution patterns, but these are documentation examples (bash commands with cp, export, pip, python) and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All detected patterns are in markdown fenced code blocks showing manual installation and usage instructions. No subprocess, os.system, or exec calls exist in the Python code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Potential Prompt Injection - Metadata Embedding
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md frontmatter includes a JSON metadata object (line 4) that specifies environment requirements (BROWSERACT_API_KEY) and binary dependencies (python). This could be used by a malicious skill to declare credential access requirements. In this skill, the declaration is accurate and transparent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern of declaring env requirements in metadata is common in skill frameworks. No obfuscation or hidden intent detected. The declaration matches actual code behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Standard CLI Argument Parser Misidentified as Cryptographic Function
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged argparse.ArgumentParser as a weak cryptographic algorithm (line 342). This is a false positive - argparse is a standard library module for parsing command-line arguments and has no relationship to cryptography.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: argparse.ArgumentParser is a well-known standard library module for CLI argument parsing. The match is a regex false positive from the static analyzer.

content.user_title: Analyze Amazon Competitors with Browser Automation
content.value_statement: Manually comparing Amazon products across multiple listings is slow and inconsistent. This skill automates competitive analysis by extracting pricing, ratings, reviews, and specifications from Amazon product pages using the BrowserAct API and generates structured comparison reports.
actual_capabilities: Extract product title, price, rating, review count, and brand from Amazon using ASIN identifiers
Compare pricing ranges and discounts across multiple competing products
Analyze review volume and average ratings to identify market leaders
Generate comparison reports in CSV, Markdown, and JSON formats
Process multiple ASINs in batch with configurable delay between requests
Identify competitive advantages and market opportunities through structured data comparison
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct.com account and API key to function
Amazon may change page structure or block automated data collection at any time
Data accuracy depends on the quality and completeness of Amazon product listings
Does not track historical price trends or provide sales volume estimates

use_cases.title: Competitive Pricing Analysis
use_cases.description: Compare current prices, original prices, and discount percentages across products from different brands in the same category to identify pricing strategies and market positioning.

use_cases.title: Product Specification Benchmarking
use_cases.description: Extract and compare technical specifications, features, and weight information across competing products to identify gaps in features or quality advantages.

use_cases.title: Seller Monitoring Dashboard
use_cases.description: Track review ratings and volumes for competitor ASINs over time to detect changes in customer satisfaction and identify emerging threats or opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Single Product Quick Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze Amazon product B09G9GB4MG and provide a summary of its price, rating, and key specifications.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick lookup of a single competitor product

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Product Competitive Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Amazon products B09G9GB4MG, B07ABC11111, and B08N5WRWNW. Generate a competitive analysis report showing price differences, rating comparisons, and specification differences.
prompt_templates.scenario: Side-by-side comparison of multiple competitor products

prompt_templates.title: Category Market Position Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze ASINs B09G9GB4MG, B07ABC11111, B08N5WRWNW, B01ABCDEFG, and B02HIJKLMN. Create a market position report identifying the lowest-priced product, highest-rated product, and biggest discount offers. Save all reports to the market-analysis folder.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive market landscape analysis with report generation

prompt_templates.title: Automated Competitor Monitoring Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a recurring analysis for ASINs B09G9GB4MG, B07ABC11111, and B08N5WRWNW. Run the analysis, compare the results against the previous report in the analysis-history folder, and highlight any pricing changes, rating shifts, or new discounts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Ongoing competitor tracking with change detection

output_examples.input: Analyze Amazon products B09G9GB4MG and B07ABC11111 and compare their pricing and ratings.
output: Analysis started for 2 ASINs. Processing B09G9GB4MG... Task submitted successfully. Waiting for completion... Data received. Processing B07ABC11111... Task submitted successfully. Waiting for completion... Data received.
- Price Leader: Brand X at $24.99 (B09G9GB4MG), followed by Brand Y at $34.99 (B07ABC11111)
- Rating Leader: Brand Y at 4.5/5 (2,847 reviews), followed by Brand X at 4.2/5 (1,203 reviews)

output_examples.input: Scrape product B09G9GB4MG and generate a detailed competitive report.
output: - Title: Wireless Bluetooth Headphones with Noise Cancellation
- Key Features: Active noise cancellation, 30-hour battery, USB-C charging, foldable design

best_practices: Validate ASIN format before running analysis to avoid failed API calls and wasted compute time
Use descriptive output directory names to keep reports organized across multiple analysis sessions
Set appropriate delay between API requests to stay within rate limits and avoid being blocked
anti_patterns: Do not analyze unrelated ASINs from different product categories together as the comparison results will be meaningless
Do not expose API keys through command-line history by using the -k flag on shared systems; use environment variables instead
Do not rely on a single analysis run for pricing decisions; market conditions change frequently

faq.question: What is an ASIN and where do I find it?
faq.answer: ASIN stands for Amazon Standard Identification Number. It is a 10-character alphanumeric code found in the product URL on Amazon. For example, in the URL amazon.com/dp/B09G9GB4MG, the ASIN is B09G9GB4MG.

faq.question: Do I need a BrowserAct account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a BrowserAct.com account and a valid API key. The skill uses BrowserAct's browser automation API to scrape Amazon product data. You can sign up at browseract.com and generate an API key from the integrations settings page.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze products from other marketplaces?
faq.answer: No, this skill is designed specifically for Amazon US product pages using the BrowserAct workflow template. It only accepts Amazon ASINs and will not work with products from eBay, Walmart, or other marketplaces.

faq.question: How long does it take to analyze one product?
faq.answer: Each product takes approximately 30 to 60 seconds to analyze. The BrowserAct workflow needs time to navigate the Amazon page, extract data, and return results. Multiple products are processed sequentially with a delay between them.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: The skill generates three output formats: CSV for spreadsheet analysis, Markdown for human-readable reports, and JSON for programmatic access. By default all three formats are generated when an output directory is specified.

faq.question: Is scraping Amazon product data allowed?
faq.answer: Amazon's Terms of Service restrict automated data collection. This skill uses a third-party browser automation service (BrowserAct) which may have its own compliance measures. You should review Amazon's ToS and consult legal counsel before using this tool commercially.
